events.title: Heavy fuel oil slick reported in San Juan Harbor (Isla Grande), Puerto Rico
events.description: An oil slick was reported in San Juan Harbor near Isla Grande, and the U.S. Coast Guard estimated roughly 3,000 gallons of heavy fuel oil on the water with an unknown source. The incident record notes the slick was fragmented and drifted southwest under northeasterly winds, and response options included dispersants, in-situ burning, and bioremediation.

location.name: San Juan Harbor (Isla Grande), Puerto Rico

sources.headline: Mystery heavy fuel oil spill reported in San Juan Harbor (Isla Grande), Puerto Rico

key_quotes.quote: estimated about 3,000 gallons of heavy fuel oil in the water, with an unknown source

events.title: Oil-saturated sediment discovered during excavation at Georgia Ports Authority Ocean Terminal (Savannah, Georgia)
events.description: During excavation work behind a river bulkhead at the Georgia Ports Authority Ocean Terminal area, responders reported finding a large volume of oil-saturated sediment. The incident record includes estimates ranging from about 10,000–20,000 gallons of oily liquid behind the bulkhead, with 2,000–4,000 gallons potentially as pure oil, and notes response coordination actions.

location.name: Savannah, Georgia (Georgia Ports Authority Ocean Terminal area)

sources.headline: Oil-saturated sediment discovered during excavation at Georgia Ports Authority Ocean Terminal (Savannah River), Savannah, Georgia

key_quotes.quote: found a large volume of oil-saturated sediment; estimates included 10,000–20,000 gallons of oily liquid

events.title: Possible hull breach and oil sheening reported for M/V Red Seagull off Galveston, Texas
events.description: While maneuvering near an Amoco pipeline spill area off Galveston, the M/V Red Seagull reportedly had bubbling and sheening observed along the hull, raising concern about a possible hull breach. The incident record notes the vessel carried crude oil and that responders planned/undertook assessment and survey activities.

sources.headline: Potential hull breach and crude oil release concern for M/V Red Seagull near Amoco pipeline spill area (lightering zone off Galveston, Texas)

key_quotes.quote: reported bubbling and sheening along the hull; responders suspected a possible hull breach

events.title: Diesel release reported after sinking of F/V Adriatic Sea north of American Samoa
events.description: Following mechanical failure, the fishing vessel F/V Adriatic Sea sank roughly 90 nautical miles north of American Samoa, and the incident record describes a catastrophic diesel release from cargo holds associated with the sinking. NOAA provided trajectory and fate/behavior support, and the record discusses estimates up to roughly 220,000 gallons at risk/released from holds.

location.name: Approx. 90 nm north of American Samoa (approx. 12°46.0′S, 170°34.92′W)

sources.headline: Diesel release following sinking of F/V Adriatic Sea approximately 90 nautical miles north of American Samoa

key_quotes.quote: catastrophic release of diesel from cargo holds occurred when the vessel sank

events.title: 1998 North American ice storm causes widespread outages and damage in Québec, Ontario, and U.S. Northeast
events.description: A prolonged freezing-rain event in early January 1998 produced extreme ice accretion across parts of Québec and Ontario and into adjacent areas of the northeastern United States. The storm caused widespread infrastructure damage and long-duration power outages.

location.name: Québec City, Québec, Canada (proxy for affected Québec/Ontario region)

sources.headline: Ice Storm 1998
NOAA repository item noaa/6384

key_quotes.quote: A prolonged freezing-rain event produced extreme ice accretion and widespread power outages.

events.title: Flash flooding in Carter County, Tennessee and nearby western North Carolina (Doe River basin)
events.description: A winter storm produced intense rainfall and rapid runoff in steep Appalachian terrain, causing destructive flash flooding in the upper Doe River basin and nearby areas overnight January 7–8, 1998.

sources.headline: Doe River Flood

key_quotes.quote: Intense rainfall and rapid runoff caused destructive flash flooding overnight Jan 7–8, 1998.

events.title: Heavy rainfall leads to minor flooding in parts of Indiana and Michigan
events.description: Heavy rains during January 3–6, 1998 led to minor flooding in portions of Indiana and Michigan, affecting multiple river basins as summarized in USGS national water conditions reporting for the month.

sources.headline: USGS National Water Conditions (Jan 1998 cover summary)

key_quotes.quote: Heavy rains during January 3–6, 1998 led to minor flooding in portions of Indiana and Michigan.

events.title: Minor flooding along the Ohio River in parts of Indiana and Illinois
events.description: Elevated river stages produced minor flooding along stretches of the Ohio River from January 14–19, 1998, as recorded in USGS national water conditions reporting for the month.

location.name: Springfield, Illinois, USA (proxy for Illinois affected corridor)

sources.headline: USGS National Water Conditions (Jan 1998 cover summary)

key_quotes.quote: Minor flooding along stretches of the Ohio River from January 14–19, 1998.

events.title: Record flooding inundates Katherine, Northern Territory after remnants of Tropical Cyclone Les
events.description: Exceptional monsoonal rainfall associated with the remnants of Tropical Cyclone Les drove record flooding in and around Katherine in late January 1998. The flooding inundated parts of the town and caused widespread disruption.

location.name: Darwin, Northern Territory, Australia (proxy for Northern Territory)

sources.headline: Australian Weather News - January 1998

sources.source_name: Australian Institute for Disaster Resilience (Knowledge Hub)
sources.headline: Flood - Katherine, Northern Territory

key_quotes.quote: Remnants of Tropical Cyclone Les contributed to exceptional monsoonal rainfall and record flooding at Katherine.

events.title: M7.5 earthquake southeast of the Loyalty Islands (near Tadine, New Caledonia)
events.description: A magnitude 7.5 earthquake was reported southeast of the Loyalty Islands near Tadine, New Caledonia. The source lists an approximate depth of about 100 km.

events.title: M6.4 earthquake in central Peru (near Pangoa)
events.description: A magnitude 6.4 earthquake was reported in central Peru near Pangoa. The source lists an approximate depth of about 33 km.

events.title: M7.1 earthquake near Antofagasta, Chile
events.description: A magnitude 7.1 earthquake was reported near Antofagasta, Chile. The source lists an approximate depth of about 42 km.

events.title: Rainforest fires and heavy smoke affect East Kalimantan, Indonesia
events.description: A report described fires burning in East Kalimantan (Borneo) producing thick smoke affecting settlements and threatening coastal towns. The report also noted that a large forest area had already been destroyed since January 1998.

sources.headline: Fire frenzy

key_quotes.quote: Fires burning through rainforest in East Kalimantan produced thick smoke

events.title: USFWS emergency rule lists San Bernardino kangaroo rat as Endangered
events.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service issued an emergency rule listing the San Bernardino kangaroo rat (Dipodomys merriami parvus) as Endangered. The rule cites severe historical habitat loss and ongoing threats such as habitat degradation and fragmentation from development and related activities in alluvial scrub and wash habitats.

location.name: Southern California (San Bernardino & Riverside Counties), United States

sources.headline: Endangered and Threatened Wildlife and Plants; Emergency Rule To List the San Bernardino Kangaroo Rat as Endangered

key_quotes.quote: Emergency Rule to List the San Bernardino Kangaroo Rat as Endangered

events.title: EPA amends Clean Air Act §112(r) Risk Management Program “List Rule” (40 CFR Part 68)
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency issued a final rule updating the list of regulated substances and threshold quantities under the Clean Air Act §112(r) Risk Management Program. The rule addresses items such as treatment of certain explosives and exemptions/clarifications for some flammables, mixtures, and specific source categories.

sources.headline: EPA final rule amends Clean Air Act §112(r) Risk Management Program “List Rule” (40 CFR Part 68)

key_quotes.quote: final rule modifying the list of regulated substances and thresholds for accidental release prevention

events.title: NMFS designates critical habitat for Umpqua River cutthroat trout (50 CFR Part 226)
events.description: NOAA’s National Marine Fisheries Service published a final rule designating critical habitat for the endangered Umpqua River cutthroat trout under the Endangered Species Act. The designation identifies specific river reaches and related areas in the Umpqua River basin, with certain exclusions noted in the rule.

location.name: Umpqua River basin, Oregon, United States

sources.headline: NMFS final rule designates critical habitat for Umpqua River cutthroat trout (50 CFR Part 226)

key_quotes.quote: final rule designating critical habitat for the endangered Umpqua River cutthroat trout

events.title: EPA authorizes revisions to Florida hazardous waste program under RCRA (40 CFR Part 271)
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency issued an immediate final rule granting final authorization for revisions to Florida’s hazardous waste management program under RCRA, subject to withdrawal if adverse comments were received. The notice provides an effective date contingent on the comment process.

sources.headline: EPA immediate final rule authorizes Florida hazardous waste management program revisions under RCRA (40 CFR Part 271)

key_quotes.quote: immediate final rule granting final authorization ... for revisions to Florida’s state hazardous waste program under RCRA

events.title: Antarctic Treaty “Madrid Protocol” enters into force
events.description: The Protocol on Environmental Protection to the Antarctic Treaty entered into force, establishing environmental principles for activities in Antarctica and designating the region as a natural reserve devoted to peace and science. The protocol includes restrictions on mineral resource activities, except for scientific research.

sources.headline: Protocol on Environmental Protection to the Antarctic Treaty enters into force
Protocol on Environmental Protection to the Antarctic Treaty (Madrid Protocol)

key_quotes.quote: entered into force, designating Antarctica as a natural reserve devoted to peace and science

events.title: EPA codifies Florida hazardous waste program in 40 CFR Part 272
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency issued an immediate final rule incorporating by reference Florida’s authorized hazardous waste program (including revisions) into 40 CFR Part 272. The codification clarifies which state hazardous waste provisions are federally enforceable under RCRA.

sources.headline: EPA immediate final rule incorporates by reference Florida’s authorized hazardous waste program into 40 CFR Part 272

key_quotes.quote: immediate final rule to codify and incorporate by reference Florida’s ... hazardous waste program ... into 40 CFR Part 272
